Name: Space@VT (Center for Space Science and Engineering Research)
Relevance: Serves as an interdisciplinary center advancing research in space weather, satellite systems, and space engineering.

Name: Kevin T. Crofton Department of Aerospace and Ocean Engineering
Relevance: The primary academic department providing education, design teams, and research in aeronautics and astronautics.

Name: Rocketry at Virginia Tech
Relevance: Engineering design team that builds and launches high-powered rockets to compete in the Intercollegiate Rocket Engineering Competition (IREC).

Name: Gobble Rockets at Virginia Tech (GRAVT)
Relevance: Introductory rocketry design team focused on teaching engineering skills through the Battle of the Rockets competition.

Name: Orbital Launch Vehicle Team (OLVT)
Relevance: Amateur organization aiming to successfully build and fly an orbital launch vehicle to place a payload into Low Earth Orbit.

Name: RockSat-X (Virginia Tech Team)
Relevance: Student team that designs and builds modular payloads for sounding rockets launched from NASA's Wallops Flight Facility.

Name: inspireFly
Relevance: Student-led engineering team dedicated to designing, building, and deploying CubeSats into low-Earth orbit.

Name: CanSat at Virginia Tech
Relevance: Designs space-capable mock payloads to compete in the AAS Cansat Competition sponsored by NASA and Lockheed Martin.

Name: Virginia CubeSat Constellation Team (Ceres)
Relevance: Collaborative student project that built and validated a 1U CubeSat deployed from the ISS to measure atmospheric drag.

Name: Puri Satellite Ground Network (PSGN)
Relevance: Operates ground stations for spacecraft communications, enabling student experiential learning in satellite data processing.

Name: Virginia Tech Ground Station (VTGS)
Relevance: Provides real-time amateur radio satellite communication and operational capabilities for university missions.

Name: Astronomy Club of Virginia Tech (ACVT)
Relevance: General interest club providing stargazing opportunities, educational outreach, and access to the university observatory.

Name: Nicholas R. Anderson Observatory
Relevance: University observatory equipped with a CDK500 system used for astronomical research and student telescope operation lessons.

Name: Field and Space Experimental Robotics (FASER) Laboratory
Relevance: Develops advanced methods for robotic collaboration, construction, and autonomous servicing on the ground and in space.

Name: Virginia Tech Astrobotics
Relevance: Multidisciplinary team designing and building a semi-autonomous rover to compete in the NASA Robotic Mining Competition.

Name: Space Drones (Space Based Robotic Platforms)
Relevance: Facility providing a combined hardware and software testbed and spacecraft simulator for distributed autonomous coordination.

Name: Drone Racing Team at Virginia Tech
Relevance: Teaches mechanical, electrical, and aerospace concepts through designing, building, programming, and racing FPV drones.

Name: Kentland Experimental Aerial Systems (KEAS) Lab / Drone Park
Relevance: Facilities enabling research collaborations and student training involving small unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs).

Name: AIAA Design, Build, Fly at Virginia Tech
Relevance: Designs, fabricates, and tests remote-controlled aircraft for the annual AIAA DBF competition.

Name: MachWorks
Relevance: Aims to design and build an autonomous supersonic fixed-wing UAV powered by turbojet engines.

Name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space (SEDS)
Relevance: Independent organization promoting space exploration and development through education, networking, and leadership.

Name: Society of Women in Aviation and Space Exploration (SWASE)
Relevance: Professional organization empowering women and minorities in aeronautic and astronautic fields through workshops and networking.

Name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) VT Branch
Relevance: Active student branch of the premier aerospace engineering professional society hosting design competitions and networking.

Name: Society of Flight Test Engineers (SFTE) VT Branch
Relevance: Student organization dedicated to the advancement of flight test engineering for aerospace vehicles.

Name: Aerospace Structures and Materials Laboratory (ASML)
Relevance: Research and educational facility dedicated to the understanding of structures and materials for aerospace applications.

Name: SuperDARN Space Weather Radar Facility
Relevance: Operates a global HF radar network to monitor and study space weather and upper atmospheric dynamics.

Name: Space Plasma Chamber
Relevance: Research facility simulating plasma and ionospheric conditions for aerospace and geospace studies.

Name: Magnetosphere–Ionosphere Science Team (MIST)
Relevance: Investigates coupled geospace dynamics and modeling of the Earth's upper atmosphere and magnetic fields.

Name: AutoBoat at Virginia Tech
Relevance: Designs fully autonomous robotic sailboats; highly adjacent to autonomous navigation and guidance systems used in aerospace.

Name: Wind Turbine Team at Virginia Tech
Relevance: Designs and tests wind turbines; relevant to aerospace concepts like aerodynamics and fluid mechanics.

Name: Society of Naval Architects and Marine Engineers (SNAME) VT
Relevance: Ocean engineering society whose studies in hydronautics closely parallel aerospace fluid dynamics.
